description: Finnish pre- and postpositions for expressing relationships between words

items.notes: Pronounced 'KANS-sa'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Ystävän kanssa' = with a friend. In spoken Finnish, often shortened to '-kaa/-kää' suffix.
examples: Tulen ystävän kanssa. — I'm coming with a friend.
Kenen kanssa puhuit? — With whom did you speak?

items.notes: Pronounced 'IL-man'. PREPOSITION: comes before the noun in partitive case. 'Ilman sokeria' = without sugar. One of the few true prepositions in Finnish.
examples: Juon kahvia ilman sokeria. — I drink coffee without sugar.
En voi elää ilman sinua. — I can't live without you.

items.notes: Pronounced 'EN-nen'. PREPOSITION: used with partitive case. 'Ennen lounasta' = before lunch. Double 'n' is long.
examples: Ennen ruokaa pesen kädet. — Before eating I wash my hands.
Tulin ennen sinua. — I came before you.

items.notes: Pronounced 'YÄL-keen'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Lounaan jälkeen' = after lunch. Double 'e' is long.
examples: Lounaan jälkeen menen ulos. — After lunch I go outside.
Työn jälkeen olen väsynyt. — After work I am tired.

items.notes: Pronounced 'AI-ka-na'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Päivän aikana' = during the day. From 'aika' (time).
examples: Kesän aikana matkustan. — During summer I travel.
Tunnin aikana opin paljon. — During the lesson I learned a lot.

items.notes: Pronounced 'SI-jaan'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Kahvin sijaan' = instead of coffee. Double 'a' is long.
examples: Juon teetä kahvin sijaan. — I drink tea instead of coffee.
Hänen sijaansa tulen minä. — Instead of him/her, I'm coming.

items.notes: Pronounced 'VAR-ten'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in partitive case. 'Sinua varten' = for you. Expresses purpose or benefit.
examples: Tämä on sinua varten. — This is for you.
Ostin lahjan häntä varten. — I bought a gift for him/her.

items.notes: Pronounced 'TA-ki-a'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Sateen takia' = because of rain. Also written 'vuoksi' (more formal).
examples: Sateen takia jäin kotiin. — Because of rain I stayed home.
Sinun takiasi olen myöhässä. — Because of you I'm late.

items.notes: Pronounced 'KOH-ti'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in partitive case. 'Kaupunkia kohti' = towards the city.
examples: Kävelen koulua kohti. — I walk towards the school.
Hän tuli minua kohti. — He/she came towards me.

items.notes: Pronounced 'O-hi'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Talon ohi' = past the house. Can also be used as an adverb: 'Bussi ajoi ohi' = The bus drove past.
examples: Kävelin kaupan ohi. — I walked past the shop.
Juna meni ohi. — The train went past.

items.notes: Pronounced 'Y-li'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Sillan yli' = over the bridge. Also means 'more than': yli sata = over a hundred.
examples: Kävelin sillan yli. — I walked over the bridge.
Olen asunut täällä yli vuoden. — I've lived here over a year.

items.notes: Pronounced 'A-li'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Sillan ali' = under the bridge (passing through). Implies movement, unlike 'alla' which is static.
examples: Juna menee sillan ali. — The train goes under the bridge.
Vene kulki sillan ali. — The boat passed under the bridge.

items.notes: Pronounced 'LÄ-pi'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Metsän läpi' = through the forest. Implies passing from one side to the other.
examples: Kävelimme metsän läpi. — We walked through the forest.
Valo paistaa ikkunan läpi. — Light shines through the window.

items.notes: Pronounced 'YM-pä-ri'. Can be a POSTPOSITION (genitive: talon ympäri = around the house) or PREPOSITION (partitive: ympäri maailmaa = around the world).
examples: Juoksin talon ympäri. — I ran around the house.
Matkustan ympäri maailmaa. — I travel around the world.

items.notes: Pronounced 'VAS-taan'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in partitive case. 'Sääntöjä vastaan' = against the rules. Double 'a' is long.
examples: Suomi pelaa Ruotsia vastaan. — Finland plays against Sweden.
Olen tätä vastaan. — I am against this.

items.notes: Pronounced 'MU-kaan'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Sääntöjen mukaan' = according to the rules. Also means 'along/with' in phrases like 'tule mukaan' = come along.
examples: Sääennusteen mukaan sataa huomenna. — According to the weather forecast it will rain tomorrow.
Tule mukaan! — Come along!

items.notes: Pronounced 'VÄ-lis-sä'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Talojen välissä' = between the houses. From 'väli' (gap/space). Double 's' is long.
examples: Puisto on talojen välissä. — The park is between the houses.
Istun teidän välissänne. — I sit between you (plural).

items.notes: Pronounced 'KES-kel-lä'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Huoneen keskellä' = in the middle of the room. From 'keski' (middle). Double 'l' is long.
examples: Pöytä on huoneen keskellä. — The table is in the middle of the room.
Keskellä yötä heräsin. — In the middle of the night I woke up.

items.notes: Pronounced 'VIE-res-sä'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Talon vieressä' = next to the house. From 'vieri' (side). Double 's' is long.
examples: Istun sinun vieressäsi. — I sit next to you.
Kauppa on pankin vieressä. — The shop is next to the bank.

items.notes: Pronounced 'PÄÄL-lä'. POSTPOSITION: comes after the noun in genitive case. 'Pöydän päällä' = on top of the table. From 'pää' (head/top). Double 'ä' and double 'l' are long.
examples: Kirja on pöydän päällä. — The book is on top of the table.
Kissa istuu sohvan päällä. — The cat sits on the sofa.
